// Physical sensing component and data flow
instance PhysicalStateSensingComponent : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Collects contextual data traces from the physical object's state; data are aggregated and abstracted for the digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PhysicalStateDataTransmitted
]

instance PhysicalStateDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PhysicalStateSensingComponent
]

instance AggregatedContextData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data derived from raw physical state traces, forming part of the digital shadow."
    DTDFVocab:fromData PhysicalStateDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ProcessingComponent
]

instance ProcessingComponent : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processing component that consumes raw traces and applies calculation or simulation models to generate derived data, preserving traceability via originates‑from associations."
    DTDFVocab:enables DigitalShadowUpdateService
]

    base:desc "Service that updates the digital shadow with derived contextual data."

]

]

    DTDFVocab:inputTo ProcessingComponent

    DTDFVocab:inputTo ProcessingComponent
]

    base:desc "MontiArc Automaton state‑machine model expressing behavioral logic."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ProcessingComponent
]

instance MachineLearningRegressorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Black‑box machine learning regressor model used for data‑driven predictions."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ProcessingComponent
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity and validity are addressed through traceability and verification activities explicitly highlighted in the documents. The reliability of a digital shadow (and thus a digital twin) depends on the correctness of its underlying models and the transformations applied, such as the SysML‑to‑simulation‑model conversion. Verification methods include systematic traceability of data traces back to source processing components and input traces, ensuring that each derived artifact can be audited. Uncertainty sources are recognised in the integration of white‑box (physics‑based) and black‑box (data‑driven) models; quality assurance involves checking model conformity to the reference model, applying verification of model transformations, and maintaining fine‑grained traceability throughout the development chain."
]
